name: Avenc de Es Mirador
equipment: Vertical progression kit, one 50 m rope (rigged to a pine tree above), one sling, at least one carabiner for the single natural-anchor deviation on a small stalagmite, three protectors
description: A typical SW-NE fracture-origin pothole in the Serra de na Burguesa with a narrow entrance (3 m x 0.5 m). The interior is formed by large piled blocks, some coated in flowstone. The ceiling is covered in white stalactites, and the angled roof has produced numerous curtain formations of varying sizes. The main chamber measures 36 x 28 m and is classified as a site of speleological interest.
full_description:
Near one of the many abandoned cart tracks found in this mountain range, 270 m above the Na Carnisera scree slope and about 350 m from the relay antennas, this small pothole is found, which has as its companion about 50 m away the Coveta de s'Ullastre (459685 E, 4377340 N).

The Avenc de Es Mirador meets all the requirements to be identified with the rest of the multiple potholes found in the Serra de na Burguesa area, formerly called the Serra de Porto Pi, since like its companions it is formed by a SW-NE fracture in the terrain. Its exact orientation is 30° N. The dimensions of its entrance are slightly less than three meters wide by fifty centimeters high.

Access to the pothole is done by sliding down a gentle and narrow ramp for a few meters until reaching the vertical that will take us to the first and only rebelay, for whose installation we will use a small stalagmite that meets the safety requirements. We will also place a protector a little below the knot. Already at this point, we will have the opportunity to observe the morphology of the cavity in which large blocks piled one upon another form the floor, some of them already covered in carbonate forming flowstones. The visit to this cavity does not give a sense of danger, as occurs in others in the area, but even so great care must be taken when moving, given the possibility that some of the many loose stones could come free, with the consequent danger for companions below.

The first formations of interest consist of a ceiling carpeted with numerous white stalactites of different sizes. The use of the rope ends at the minus twenty-five meter mark, where the ground becomes more stable and level while the large blocks are above us, so we can move "comfortably" through the cavity, scrutinizing its corners and finding interesting formations in some of them. The incline of the pothole's ceiling has caused the formation of numerous curtain formations of all types and sizes, not to mention the stalagmites and stalactites.

The deepest part of the cave is full of small stones that prevent seeing whether the cavity continues, despite it not giving that impression.

In summary, as I said at the beginning of this description, the Avenc de Es Mirador is faithful to the style of those in this area of the island — characteristics that make many regular practitioners of this activity dislike visiting them due to the fear instilled by the quantity of unstable blocks piled up inside. The concretions are not excessively abundant, but there are enough to keep us entertained for a while taking photographs before beginning the ascent. The dimensions of the main level are 36 m long by slightly less than 28 m at its widest point.

The Avenc de Es Mirador is considered a site of speleological interest.
